title: Fiscal Federalism & the 15th FC
lead: The core objective: ==match resources with responsibilities==, promote equity, and maintain macroeconomic stability. In a nation as diverse as India, fiscal federalism is the ==economic glue== binding regions to a collective national vision.

items.title: Challenges
bullets: ==Vertical imbalance==: states spend big but tax little → dependence on transfers
==Horizontal imbalance==: wide fiscal-capacity disparities among states
==CSS proliferation== encroaches on state subjects and rigidifies spending
==Rising cess & surcharges== — not shareable — shrink the divisible pool
Compliance costs of central laws; ==central approval for borrowing==
Inconsistent fiscal data across states

items.title: Solutions
bullets: ==Rationalise CSS== — fewer schemes, more untied transfers
==Expand the divisible pool== — limit cesses or compensate states
Strengthen state revenue base; share buoyant taxes down to local level
Strengthen ==SFCs==; independent ==fiscal councils==; better data
Align borrowing limits with sustainability; flexibility for productive investment
==Outcome-based transfers== balanced with equity

blocks.text: States' share of central taxes: ==41% for 2021–26== (down from 42% to account for J&K and Ladakh becoming UTs).

items.label: forest & ecology + tax and fiscal effort

head: Grant
Amount
rows: ₹1.3 lakh crore — health, education, agriculture, judiciary, aspirational districts, roads
₹49,599 crore — social needs, governance, water, culture, tourism
₹4.36 lakh crore incl. performance-linked and health grants

items: ==Fiscal roadmap==: Centre's fiscal deficit to ==4% of GDP by 2025-26==; states to ==3% of GSDP by 2023-26==; extra ==0.5% GSDP borrowing== for power-sector reforms; GST rationalisation, stronger property tax, fiscal transparency.

items.title: Strengths
bullets: ==Objective devolution criteria== balancing equity (income distance, demographic performance) and efficiency (tax effort)
Sector- and state-specific grants for unique challenges
Fiscal discipline, transparency, GST/property-tax rationalisation
Strengthened ==local bodies and disaster funding==

items.title: Criticisms
bullets: ==Shrinking divisible pool== — cesses/surcharges erode states' effective share
==2011 Census + demographic criteria== discontented southern states that controlled population
==Scope creep== in ToR (performance incentives, defence funding) diluted focus
==Conditional local-body grants== limit flexibility
Limited progress on CSS rationalisation and ==state fiscal autonomy==

sections.title: Before the 16th FC: States' Fiscal Health

items: ==Challenges ahead==: persistent vertical/horizontal imbalances; shrinking divisible pool; widening regional disparities; weak ==urban local government finance== post-GST; climate mandates; poor data standardisation.

==Health determinants==: revenue mobilisation vs GSDP; quality (developmental/capital share) of expenditure; deficit ratios; ==debt-to-GSDP and interest burden==; expenditure efficiency; transparency; institutional strength (SFCs, FRLs).
==Reforms undertaken by states==: FRLs, medium-term fiscal statements, DBT, GST adoption, pension reforms, tax-administration modernisation, market borrowings, expenditure rationalisation.
==Enhance revenue mobilisation== — broaden base, property tax, digital compliance
==Improve expenditure efficiency== — outcome & climate budgeting, rationalise subsidies
==Fiscal transparency== — publish off-budget borrowings and contingent liabilities
==Strengthen local bodies== — autonomy, timely transfers, capacity
==Innovative financing== — green bonds, blended finance, PPPs
